perturbation.original_text: The Company shall provide all Content to HDI to be included in the Site no later than ten (10) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company should aim to provide Content to HDI for inclusion on the Site, ideally within approximately ten (10) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a firm deadline for content provision. The modified text replaces 'shall provide' with 'should aim to provide' and 'no later than ten (10) days' with 'ideally within approximately ten (10) days'. This change introduces ambiguity by making the deadline non-mandatory and the timeframe approximate, rendering it difficult to enforce. The term 'approximately' is not definitive or quantifiable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of Contract - New Jersey Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification changes a definite obligation into a suggestion. In New Jersey, a breach of contract occurs when a party fails to perform a contractual duty. By making the content delivery deadline ambiguous, it becomes difficult to establish a clear breach if the content is delivered late or not at all, undermining the enforceability of this provision under New Jersey contract law. The original text created a concrete obligation, while the modified version is a mere aspiration.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens a firm deadline into an ambiguous suggestion, thereby undermining the enforceability of the original provision. The original text uses 'shall provide' and 'no later than ten (10) days', which create a clear obligation. The changed text uses 'should aim to provide' and 'ideally within approximately ten (10) days', introducing ambiguity. The Law Explanation states that a breach of contract occurs when a party fails to perform a contractual duty and that this change makes it difficult to establish a clear breach. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 generally covers commercial transactions and includes sections on contract formation and modification (e.g., 12A:2-209), it doesn't explicitly define 'breach of contract.' However, the overall effect of the change introduces ambiguity that could make the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Payments to HDI shall be due monthly within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar month and shall be accompanied by documentation reasonably detailing the calculation of the payment.
perturbation.changed_text: Payments to HDI are generally expected to be remitted monthly, with a target of thirty (30) days following the close of each calendar month, alongside documentation offering a general overview of the payment calculation.
perturbation.explanation: The original language established a clear payment due date and documentation requirement. By changing 'shall be due' to 'are generally expected', 'within thirty (30) days' to 'with a target of thirty (30) days', and 'documentation reasonably detailing' to 'documentation offering a general overview', the text becomes ambiguous. The payment obligation becomes less defined, and the standard for payment documentation is significantly weakened.
perturbation.law_explanation: The New Jersey Prompt Payment Act mandates timely payment for work performed. By making the payment terms vague, the modified text introduces the possibility of delayed payments and inadequate documentation, making it difficult for HDI to enforce its right to prompt payment under the Act. The phrase 'generally expected' suggests that late payments might be acceptable, and a 'general overview' of calculations provides little assurance of accuracy or completeness, contradicting the spirit and intent of the Prompt Payment Act, which is designed to ensure timely and accurate payments.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: New Jersey Revised Statutes Section 2A:30A-2 (2022) - Payment to prime contractor, subcontractor, subsubcontractor, timely payment; exceptions; disputes; resolution. :: 2022 New Jersey Revised Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 N.J. Rev. Stat. 2023 N.J. Rev. Stat. 2022 N.J. Rev. Stat. (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 New Jersey Revised Statutes Title 2A - Administration of Civil and Criminal Justice Section 2A:30A-2 - Payment to prime contractor, subcontractor, subsubcontractor, timely payment; exceptions; disputes; resolution. Universal Citation: NJ Rev Stat § 2A:30A-2 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next 2A:30A-2 Payment to prime contractor, subcontractor, subsubcontractor, timely payment; exceptions; disputes; resolution. 2. a. If a prime contractor has performed in accordance with the provisions of a contract with the owner and the billing for the work has been approved and certified by the owner or the owner's authorized approving agent, the owner shall pay the amount due to the prime contractor for each periodic payment, final payment or retainage monies not more than 30 calendar days after the billing date, which for a periodic billing, shall be the periodic billing date specified in the contract. The billing shall be deemed approved and certified 20 days after the owner receives it unless the owner provides, before the end of the 20-day period, a written statement of the amount withheld and the reason for withholding payment, except that in the case of a public or governmental entity that requires the entity's governing body to vote on authorizations for each periodic payment, final payment or retainage monies, the amount due may be approved and certified at the next scheduled public meeting of the entity's governing body, and paid during the entity's subsequent payment cycle, provided this exception has been defined in the bid specifications and contract documents. b. If a subcontractor or subsubcontractor has performed in accordance with the provisions of its contract with the prime contractor or subcontractor and the work has been accepted by the owner, the owner's authorized approving agent, or the prime contractor, as applicable, and the parties have not otherwise agreed in writing, the prime contractor shall pay to its subcontractor and the subcontractor shall pay to its subsubcontractor within 10 calendar days of the receipt of each periodic payment, final payment or receipt of retainage monies, the full amount received for the work of the subcontractor or subsubcontractor based on the work completed or the services rendered under the applicable contract. In the case of ongoing work on the same project for which partial payments are made, the amount of money owed for work already completed shall only be payable if the subcontractor or subsubcontractor is performing to the satisfaction of the prime contractor or subcontractor, as applicable. c. If a payment due pursuant to the provisions of this section is not made in a timely manner, the delinquent party shall be liable for the amount of money owed under the contract, plus
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding payment terms, potentially conflicting with the New Jersey Prompt Payment Act, as explained in the Law Explanation. The original text specified that payments 'shall be due' within 30 days, whereas the modified text states payments are 'generally expected' with a 'target of thirty (30) days'. This introduces uncertainty, potentially allowing for delayed payments and disputes, undermining the Act's intent to ensure timely payments. Scraped Law Snippet 1, N.J. Rev. Stat. § 2A:30A-2, indicates that the owner 'shall pay the amount due to the prime contractor...not more than 30 calendar days after the billing date', which is a much stronger mandate than the 'generally expected' language in the modified contract. The change weakens the payment obligation, creating a legal contradiction under the Ambiguities definition.
